title: Hearing for Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act Scheduled For Wednesday 3/10
author: James Silberman
orientation: A short news alert announcing that Missouri's SB391, the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act, was scheduled for a March 10, 2021 hearing in the Senate Health and Pensions Committee. The post urges Missourians to contact the seven committee members and lists their phone numbers and email addresses.
audiences: Pro-life voter weighing the abolition vs. incremental approach

questions.question: What bill is being heard, when, and in which committee?
questions.answer: Missouri's SB391, titled the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act, was scheduled to be heard on Wednesday, March 10 at noon in the Missouri Senate Health and Pensions Committee. The article frames it as the second abolition bill in 2021 to actually receive a hearing.
questions.quote: The Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act (SB391) is scheduled to be heard in the Missouri Senate Health and Pensions Committee on Wednesday, March 10 at Noon. It will be the second abolition bill to receive a hearing thus far in 2021.

questions.question: Who authored SB391 and what is his political backstory?
questions.answer: State Senator Mike Moon authored SB391. He won his Senate seat by defeating former Missouri Republican Party Chairman David Cole in the 2020 GOP primary on an explicit abolitionist platform, then ran unopposed in the general election. Cole had publicly attacked Moon for being too anti-abortion.
questions.quote: The bill’s author, Mike Moon, was elected to the State Senate last year, defeating former Missouri Republican Party Chairman David Cole in the Republican primary and running unopposed in the general election. Moon ran on a platform of abolishing abortion, while Cole ran as anti-abolitionist, even writing an op-ed in a Missouri newspaper accusing Moon of being too anti-abortion.

questions.question: Is this the first time Moon has tried to pass an abolition bill in Missouri?
questions.answer: No. SB391 is identical to legislation Moon previously filed during his time in the Missouri State House, and he refiled it in January 2021 after being elected to the Senate. However, March 10 was the first time the bill itself had ever received a hearing.
questions.quote: In January, Moon kept his word and filed SB391, a bill identical to legislation he had previously filed during his time in the Missouri State House. Wednesday’s hearing will be the first time it has been heard.

questions.question: What does the article ask Missouri readers to actually do before the hearing?
questions.audience: Pro-life voter weighing the abolition vs. incremental approach
questions.answer: It asks every Missourian to call and email all seven members of the Senate Health and Pensions Committee and provides their direct phone numbers and Senate email addresses, including Republicans Onder, Koenig, Eigel, Rehder, and White and Democrats Schupp and Washington.
questions.quote: All Missourians should call and email the seven members of the committee.

questions.question: How does the article suggest readers stay informed if they want to track abolition bill news beyond this hearing?
questions.answer: Free the States tells readers their social media reach has been throttled and directs them to sign up for email updates so they don't miss news on abolition bills, framing direct email as the reliable channel rather than algorithm-mediated platforms.
questions.quote: Big tech is cracking down on our ability to reach you! Our social media reach has been greatly decreased in recent months. To make sure you are not missing important content and news about abolition bills from Free the States, [sign up for email updates](https://abolitionistsrising.us19.list-manage.com/subscribe?u=6be105194fd5a5cf21634473a&id=bb5a76c165)!
